name: multipass
description: Create, control and connect to Ubuntu instances

name: -h
--help
options.description: Displays help on commandline options

options.name: --help-all
options.description: Displays help including Qt specific options

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Increase logging verbosity. Repeat the 'v' in the short option for more detail. Maximum verbosity is obtained with 4 (or more) v's, i.e. -vvvv

args.name: definition
args.description: Alias definition in the form <instance>:<command>

args.name: name
args.description: Name given to the alias being defined, defaults to <command>

options.name: --format
options.description: Output list in the requested format. Valid formats are: table (default), json, csv and yaml

args.name: name
args.description: Name of instances to delete

options.name: --all
options.description: Delete all instances

name: -p
--purge
options.description: Purge instances immediately

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Run a command on an instance

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the instance to run the command on

args.name: command
args.description: Command to execute on the instance

subcommands.name: find
subcommands.description: Display available images to create instances from

args.name: string
args.description: An optional value to search for in [<remote:>]<string> format, where <remote> can be either 'release' or 'daily'. If <remote> is omitted, it will search 'release' first, and if no matches are found, it will then search 'daily'. <string> can be a partial image hash or an Ubuntu release version, codename or alias.

options.name: --format
options.description: Output list in the requested format. Valid formats are: table (default), json, csv and yaml

options.name: --show-unsupported
options.description: Show unsupported cloud images as well

args.name: key
args.description: Path to the setting whose configured value should be obtained

options.name: --raw
options.description: Output in raw format. For now, this affects only the representation of empty values (i.e. "" instead of "<empty>")

args.name: command
args.description: Name of command to display help for

args.name: name
args.description: Names of instances to display information about

options.name: --format
options.description: Output list in the requested format. Valid formats are: table (default), json, csv and yaml

options.name: --all
options.description: Display info for all instances

subcommands.name: launch
subcommands.description: Create and start an Ubuntu instance

args.name: image
args.description: Optional image to launch. If omitted, then the default Ubuntu LTS will be used

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the command to complete. Note that some background operations may continue beyond that. By default, instance startup and initialization is limited to 5 minutes each

name: -c
--cpus
options.description: Number of CPUs to allocate. Minimum: 1, default: 1

name: -d
--disk
options.description: Disk space to allocate. Positive integers, in bytes, or with K, M, G suffix Minimum: 512M, default: 5G.

name: -m
--mem
options.description: Amount of memory to allocate. Positive integers, in bytes, or with K, M, G suffix Minimum: 128M, default: 1G.

name: -n
--name
options.description: Name for the instance. If it is 'primary' (the configured primary instance name), the user's home directory is mounted inside the newly launched instance, in 'Home'

options.name: --cloud-init
options.description: Path to a user-data cloud-init configuration, or '-' forstdin

options.name: --network
options.description: Add a network interface to the instance, where <spec> is in the "key=value,key=value" format

options.name: --bridged
options.description: Adds one `--network bridged` network

options.name: --format
options.description: Output list in the requested format. Valid formats are: table (default), json, csv and yaml

subcommands.name: mount
subcommands.description: Mount a local directory in the instance

args.name: source
args.description: Path to the local directory to mount

args.name: target
args.description: Target mount points, in <name>[:<path>] format, where <name> is an instance name, and optional <path> is the mount point. If omitted, the mount point will be the same as the source's absolute path

name: -g
--gid-map
options.description: A mapping of group IDs for use in the mount. File and folder ownership will be mapped from <host> to <instance> inside the instance. Can be used multiple times

name: -u
--uid-map
options.description: A mapping of user IDs for use in the mount. File and folder ownership will be mapped from <host> to <instance> inside the instance. Can be used multiple times

options.name: --format
options.description: Output list in the requested format. Valid formats are: table (default), json, csv and yaml

args.name: name
args.description: Names of instances to recover

options.name: --all
options.description: Recover all deleted instances

args.name: name
args.description: Names of instances to restart. If omitted, and without the --all option, 'primary' will be assumed

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the command to complete. Note that some background operations may continue beyond that. By default, instance startup and initialization is limited to 5 minutes each

options.name: --all
options.description: Restart all instances

args.name: key=value
args.description: A key-value pair. The key specifies a path to the setting to configure. The value is its intended value

subcommands.name: shell
subcommands.description: Open a shell on a running instance

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the instance to open a shell on. If omitted, 'primary' (the configured primary instance name) will be assumed. If the instance is not running, an attempt is made to start it (see `start` for more info)

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the command to complete. Note that some background operations may continue beyond that. By default, instance startup and initialization is limited to 5 minutes each

args.name: name
args.description: Names of instances to start. If omitted, and without the --all option, 'primary' (the configured primary instance name) will be assumed. If 'primary' does not exist but is included in a successful start command either implicitly or explicitly), it is launched automatically (see`launch` for more info).

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the command to complete. Note that some background operations may continue beyond that. By default, instance startup and initialization is limited to 5 minutes each

options.name: --all
options.description: Start all instances

args.name: name
args.description: Names of instances to stop. If omitted, and without the --all option, 'primary' will be assumed.

options.name: --all
options.description: Stop all instances

name: -t
--time
options.description: Time from now, in minutes, to delay shutdown of the instance

name: -c
--cancel
options.description: Cancel a pending delayed shutdown

args.name: name
args.description: Names of instances to suspend. If omitted, and without the --all option, 'primary' will be assumed.

options.name: --all
options.description: Suspend all instances

subcommands.name: transfer
subcommands.description: Transfer files between the host and instances

args.name: source
args.description: One or more paths to transfer, prefixed with <name:> for paths inside the instance, or '-' for stdin

args.name: destination
args.description: The destination path, prefixed with <name:> for paths inside the instance, or '-' for stdout

subcommands.name: umount
subcommands.description: Unmount a directory from an instance

args.name: mount
args.description: Mount points, in <name>[:<path>] format, where <name> are instance names, and optional <path> are mount points. If omitted, all mounts will be removed from the named instances.

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the alias to remove

options.name: --format
options.description: Output list in the requested format. Valid formats are: table (default), json, csv and yaml
